titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 67

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=132 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:28:02.780117+00 | translation moa=133 status=live modified=2016-06-07 15:02:50.615886+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis . clericis ⁊ laicis presentibus ⁊ futuris . Malcolmus filius Duncani Comitis
de fif . Salutem . Sciant tam posteri quam presentes . me concessisse ⁊ hac mea carta confirmasse
deo ⁊ sancto Andree ⁊ priori ⁊ conuentui apud sanctam Andream deo seruientibus ⁊ seruitu
ris
donationes quas Duncanus Comes pater meus fecit illis . scilicet . ecclesiam de Cupre . ecclesiam de Mar
king . ecclesiam de Sconin . Capellam de katel . cum omnibus pertinentiis suis . Quare uolo . ut predic
tus prior ⁊ conuentus prioratus ecclesias teneant ⁊ possideant de me ⁊ de heredibus meis .
ita libere ⁊ quiete  sicut carte patris mei testantur . Testibus . Merleswano . Adam de Syrais .
Adam fratre Comitis . Alano filio Symonis . Odone senescaldo . Willelmo de lacel’ . Waltero
de lundin . hugone pincerna . Albino capellano . Milone de Raiuill’ . Gaufrido de
Malleuill’ . ⁊ aliis quampluribus .

To all sons of Holy Mother Church, clerk and lay, present and future, Malcolm, son of Duncan earl of Fife, sends Greeting: Let those to come as well as those present know that I have granted and by this my charter made firm, to God and to Saint Andrew, and to the prior and religious community serving God at St Andrews now and in the future, the donations that Duncan the Earl, my father, made to them, that is, the church of Cupar, the church of Markinch, the church of Scoonie, the chapel of Kettle, with everything belonging to them. Wherefore I will that the aforesaid prior and community of the priory should hold and possess the churches of me and my heirs, as freely and quietly as the charters of my father bear witness. As witnesses: Merleswain; Adam of Ceres; Adam the Earl’s brother; Alan son of Simon; Odo the steward; William de Lascelles; Walter of Lundin; Hugh the butler; Albin the chaplain; Milo de Raiville; Geoffrey de Melville; and many others.
